mappings.rationale: Directly covers major energy systems, conversion devices, and power generation technologies outlined throughout this section.
Covers nuclear fission reactors and nuclear-powered generators, key topics under devices for utilizing nuclear energy and electric power generation.
Directly addresses recurring energy sources including solar, wind, water, and biomass energy, and the devices for their utilization.
Relevant to primary energy sources including thermonuclear reaction, nuclear fission, and radioactivity.
Covers radioactivity as a primary energy source and radioactive isotopes as devices for utilizing nuclear energy.
Provides the fundamental physics underlying energy conversion, heat exchangers, and thermoelectric devices covered in this section.
Covers the engineering principles behind machines, turbines, and power generation systems described in the section.
Relevant to optoelectronic devices, semiconductor lasers, and lighting devices covered in the section's energy concentration and control topics.
Covers magnets and electromagnets, which are key devices for utilizing magnetic and electrical energy.
Relevant to advanced electrical energy conversion and solid-state devices for energy concentration.
Covers materials science relevant to solid-state devices, semiconductors, and integrated circuits for energy concentration and control.
Relevant to solid-state devices including transistors, semiconductor diodes, and integrated circuits used in energy control.
Covers the physics of wind and water turbines, hydraulic devices, and steam turbines used in energy utilization.
Relevant to the environmental impact of nonrenewable energy sources (coal, natural gas, oil) and the shift to renewable energy.
Addresses the sustainability of energy systems, including the transition from nonrenewable to recurring energy sources.
Relevant to heat energy utilization and the combustion processes underlying internal-combustion engines and chemical energy devices.
Covers the chemical principles underlying batteries, fuel cells, and chemical energy conversion devices.
